name: Vesak
shortDescription: Vesak, also known as Buddha Jayanti, is a significant Buddhist festival commemorating the birth, enlightenment (Buddhahood), and death (Parinirvana) of Gautama Buddha.
description: Vesak is the most important festival in Buddhism, observed by Buddhists around the world. It commemorates the birth, enlightenment, and death of Siddhartha Gautama, the historical Buddha. These three events are believed to have occurred on the same day of the year, during the full moon of the lunar month of Vesakha, typically falling in May or June. The observance of Vesak varies across different Buddhist traditions and countries, but it generally involves visiting temples, offering prayers, meditating, listening to sermons, and engaging in acts of charity. Many Buddhists choose to abstain from meat on Vesak, and some release caged animals as a symbolic act of liberation. The day is an opportunity for Buddhists to reaffirm their commitment to the teachings of the Buddha and to strive for inner peace and compassion. The celebration emphasizes the core Buddhist values of mindfulness, non-violence, and the pursuit of wisdom. It's also a time for reflection on the impermanence of life and the importance of practicing the Eightfold Path. The significance of Vesak stems from its connection to the foundational narrative of Buddhism and the life of the Buddha. By remembering and celebrating these key events, Buddhists strengthen their connection to the tradition and are inspired to follow the Buddha's path towards enlightenment. Furthermore, Vesak serves as a reminder of the interconnectedness of all beings and the importance of cultivating compassion and understanding in the world. The festival plays a crucial role in preserving and promoting Buddhist culture and values, fostering a sense of community and shared identity among Buddhists worldwide.

sources.title: In the Buddha's Words: An Anthology of Discourses from the Pali Canon
sources.author: Bhikkhu Bodhi
